name: Escletxa de sa Fusta
starting_point: Upper part of the Costa d'en Blanes urbanization
clothing: A good set of overalls recommended
equipment: One 50 m rope, 2 bolts, a cord for natural belay, 3 carabiners, some rope protectors
description: A small but engaging vertical cave discovered in October 2011 on the Serra de na Burguesa, near the firebreak path to the antenna installations. The entirely vertical fracture is never wider than one meter with sections as narrow as 40 cm, reaching 25 m depth. Formations are sparse but present throughout, mainly coralloid and pavement-type, with stalactites only near the entrance. The ascent can be quite challenging due to the extreme narrowness.
full_description:
Location

On October 30, 2011, while walking through the area in the company of A. Villar, we located the cavity. Once we had taken its coordinates, we left with the intention of beginning its exploration as soon as possible, which I did in the company of T. Mateu on November 1, All Saints' Day.

Description

The "Escletxa de sa Fusta" is a small cavity of -25 m that nonetheless meets the necessary requirements for spending a good time practicing sport caving. Located on the firebreak of the path leading to the antennas, just a few meters from the trail, its approach, while not short, is relatively comfortable.

Like other cavities of the "Serra de na Burguesa," the "Escletxa de sa Fusta" is a completely vertical fracture with a maximum width that in no case exceeds one meter, where in some places we will have to overcome constrictions no wider than 40 cm.

Sparse in formations, they are nonetheless found throughout the cavity, especially coralloid parietal and pavement types; stalactites, on the other hand, are only visible near the entrance mouth.

We can fix the rope to the thickest pine tree, the one located in the direction of the antennas. We will then enter the pit and look for the spit where we will set up a rebelay. The descent through this part of the cavity presents no major problem except that at around ten and a half meters, when we reach the level of the first rock bridge, we will have to pendulum a little to settle onto it. This can be a good place to leave backpacks and other accessories we won't need — in fact, it is the only place to do so. A spit invites us to set up another rebelay, but it is not necessary; we can continue descending another three meters to a new rock bridge. Once past it, two meters further down, a new spit allows us to set up a second rebelay to continue descending until we reach a point where we can rig on a rock bridge to finish the descent. We will still have to step on a stone along the way, unless it has come loose from being stepped on by another caver. The ascent can be truly challenging due to the narrowness.
